diagrams.title: One-command closure authority and evidence boundaries
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Operator["Explicit guarded command"] --> Session["Guarded Run session"]
  Session --> Worktree["Managed worktree implementation owner"]
  Session --> Review["Independent read-only reviewer"]
  Worktree --> Verify["Verification owners"]
  Review --> Repair["Finding-preserving repair loop"]
  Verify --> Bind["Current HEAD evidence binding"]
  Repair --> Bind
  Bind --> Gate["pr-prepare Gate SSOT"]
  Gate --> Ready["pr_ready typed terminal"]
  Ambiguous["Material ambiguity"] -->|bounded choices only| Human["Human decision checkpoint"]
  Session -. excludes .-> External["PR create, merge, waiver, material external side effect"]
  Stale["Stale HEAD, malformed decision, unknown action"] -->|fail closed| Stop["Typed stop reason"]

clauses.statement: execute run with --until pr-ready and --autonomy guarded selects the closed autonomous implementation DAG and the persisted codex then claude-code provider order by default, while an explicit legacy profile and provider override remain available and auditable.

test_refs.case: OCR-T-1 Default one-command profile and public help

clauses.statement: Only final_prepare may produce pr_ready, and it does so only after the current managed-worktree HEAD pr-prepare artifact reports gate_status.ready_for_pr_create=true.

The guarded closure never creates or merges a PR, grants a critical waiver, or performs a material external side effect; it returns a typed stop or leaves those operations to an explicit human command.

The guarded Run workflow state matrix covers success, restart and resume, material human decision, verification failure with explicit correction/resume, needs_changes review repair convergence, no progress, quota, timeout, CI pending, and cancellation; every transition ends in current-HEAD pr_ready or an explicit typed stop. Operator cancellation commits terminal Run authority before containing every active runtime dispatch; stale in-flight dispatch or poll completion preserves cancelled authority and cannot continue the remaining action suffix. A material ambiguity returns a bounded human_decision descriptor containing type, question, choices, material_reason, impact_scope, source_refs, and stop_node_id. The safe-action transport rejects a missing or malformed descriptor fail-closed; guarded-run-session validates an accepted descriptor through createHumanDecision, persists the decision artifact and pending_decision, and resume journals the exact answer and reflected_in paths before restarting prepare_artifacts.

A production connector smoke proves a real managed-worktree commit plus distinct read-only closed review session when the provider exposes the required capability. If every selected provider lacks a required capability, it instead proves a pre-mutation typed stop that binds provider, missing capability, and recovery boundary to the Run; the available-provider commit/review path remains covered by production-shaped E2E and is not represented as a real provider execution.

test_refs.case: repaired HEAD invalidates the prior review operation checkpoint
OCR-T-3 Runtime commit, review, and repair convergence
OCR-T-5 Production smoke and dogfood evidence

clauses.statement: Roadmap closure reconciles the already merged Autonomous Action DAG, Production Runtime Connectors, and Independent Review Orchestration Story lifecycles from PR #372, PR #377, and PR #382 without reimplementing them. The initial PR candidate keeps the final Story and parent roadmap active; after its current-HEAD Gate and imported CI evidence, a focused closure commit marks them completed and is itself reverified, rereviewed, and re-imported from CI so it is mergeable. Explicit execute merge consumes that pre-merge acceptance and supplies delivery confirmation in pr-merge.json and the canonical audit; post-merge confirmation is not a circular pre-PR Gate prerequisite.

inherited_behaviors.condition: PR #377 and PR #382 are already merged and their implementation surfaces are outside this closure Story

test_refs.case: OCR-T-5 Production smoke and dogfood evidence
